title: Calanthe gustavii
taxonomy: Calanthe gustavii is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest and most diverse family of flowering plants. Within the Orchidaceae family, Calanthe gustavii is part of the subfamily Epidendroideae. The genus Calanthe is a group of orchids that are commonly known as the 'grass-pinks' due to their grass-like growth habit. Calanthe gustavii specifically is a type of terrestrial orchid native to tropical Asia.

intro: Calanthe gustavii is a species of orchid, commonly known as the Gustav's calanthe, that is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity, which can contribute to its potential health benefits such as improving cardiovascular health and reducing the risk of chronic diseases.
